commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed after the /bin/sh update; maintainers need to decide whether to require live WSL2 readback proof or accept the blocked Crabbox capacity gap.; Cleared: The diff adds a security-sensitive shell bridge, but the command string is static, the tokenized value stays on stdin, and no new dependency, permission, or global shell execution path was introduced. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WSL2-only `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` clipboard bridge and tests that tokenized dashboard URLs stay on stdin while non-WSL fallbacks remain unchanged.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +40. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. we do not have a high-confidence live reproduction in this review. The source path is clear from current main and the linked WSL2 report, but the PR body says the real WSL2 readback attempt never received a lease.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed after the `/bin/sh` update; maintainers need to decide whether to require live WSL2 readback proof or accept the blocked Crabbox capacity gap.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv, and avoid Bash login/profile startup
- preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts` - passed, 30 focused tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - completed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check` / `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.86
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.82
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label wsl-clipboard-88365-check-changed --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- 'pnpm check:changed'` - passed on AWS Crabbox `cbx_3b0ce62e5de1`, run `run_b224b20cb174`; lease stopped cleanly
- WSL2 Crabbox proof attempt: `run_cc06cc1779c9`, Azure `windows/wsl2`, class `standard`, market `on-demand`, slug `wsl-clipboard-88365-bridge`; coordinator lease allocation did not return a lease after about 8.5 minutes, so the current-task local process was stopped before clipboard readback could run

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution or a Bash login shell. The WSL2 path uses `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin.

Real environment tested: local focused unit/link tests in a Codex worktree; AWS Crabbox Linux `pnpm check:changed`; autoreview of the local patch and final committed branch. A live Azure Windows WSL2 Crabbox run was attempted but never received a lease from the coordinator.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, formatter, lint, diff-check, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` commands listed above. The WSL2 Crabbox attempt requested an Azure `windows/wsl2` lease and would have piped a generated probe string through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'`, then read it back with PowerShell `Get-Clipboard`.

Evidence after fix: `copyToClipboard` now prepends the startup-free shell bridge only when `isWSL2Sync()` is true. The WSL2 regression test asserts the token URL is passed via stdin, not argv. A separate fallback-order test asserts non-WSL2 environments still try `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, then `clip.exe` without the WSL2 bridge.

Observed result after fix: focused tests, lint, diff-check, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` all passed on commit `0c1aa2b5b91b4d6dce4cf4598fb043db9d05b9e7`. Live WSL2 readback is still blocked on runner provisioning, not on a failing clipboard readback.

What was not tested: successful live WSL2 clipboard round-trip on a real Windows host; Azure Crabbox WSL2 lease allocation did not produce a VM/lease for this run.
